title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Spiral Review
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a bounded Git automation and trust policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Hook Lifecycle evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Hook Lifecycle assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Hook Lifecycle has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Hook Lifecycle precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Cloning a repository installs and runs all versioned hooks automatically.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories. Artifact under review: a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Safe Directory Ownership, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Safe Directory Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.

checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
